description: Generated from init-orch.md. Do not edit directly.

project.name: your-repo
project.summary: Describe what this repository does and what a successful change should optimize for.
project.mission: Keep human and agent work aligned around practical, reviewable improvements.
successCriteria: Keep guidance easy for humans to update.

responsibilities: Propose the smallest safe path forward.

roles.role: Make focused code changes and verify the result.
Review diffs for bugs, regressions, and missing checks.
responsibilities: Look for correctness and safety issues.
Call out missing tests or risky assumptions.

roles.role: Assess how the human-agent workflow is performing over time.
responsibilities: Identify friction in prompts, permissions, and handoffs.
Recommend changes to the orchestration design.

rules: Ask before destructive or irreversible actions.
Do not edit secrets or production credentials.

workflow.planningTrigger: Plan first for non-trivial or ambiguous changes.
workflow.implementationFocus: Prefer the smallest safe change that improves the repository.
escalation: Escalate before destructive actions or production-impacting changes.
stopConditions: Stop when missing credentials or required access blocks progress.
Stop when the requested action would be unsafe without human review.

required: Run focused tests or explain why none were available.

skills.when: Use for multi-step work that benefits from a short implementation plan.
steps: Summarize the goal and important constraints.
List the checks needed before finishing.

evaluation.cadence: Review after meaningful project changes or when the workflow feels brittle.
signals: Humans repeatedly have to restate context.
Agents stall because permissions are too weak or too broad.
goals: Improve safety without making normal work painful.
evaluation.recommendationFormat: Short actionable recommendations grouped by risk and ergonomics.
